name: Temple Valley
desc: Valley subdivision in the Kahaluu area — named for nearby Valley of the Temples, quiet residential streets, mountain views.
insider: Byodo-In Temple at the back of Valley of the Temples Memorial Park is a stunning replica of a 900-year-old Japanese temple — entry is $5
The neighborhood is very residential and quiet — mostly single-family homes from the 1960s-70s on peaceful cul-de-sacs
Morning mist rolling through the valley with the Ko'olau cliffs towering above is breathtaking — bring a camera before 8am
It's one of the more affordable windward valleys with easy access to Kamehameha Highway and the H-3 freeway
The peacocks at Byodo-In Temple roam freely and their calls echo through the valley — beautiful but loud at dawn
Temple Valley Shopping Center has basic services — Zippy's and a few local spots cover everyday food needs
The neighborhood is tucked far enough into the valley that it feels rural, but Kaneohe town is only 5 minutes away
Ko'olau cliff waterfalls appear after heavy rains — you can see dozens of temporary waterfalls cascading down the green walls
Home prices here are among the lowest in the windward corridor for decent single-family houses with mountain views
The Haiku Road / Kahekili Highway intersection can back up badly during rush hour — use Temple Valley Road as an alternate
